T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries may use brute force attacks to obtain credentials.

T1566.name: Phishing
T1566.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.

T1053.name: Scheduled Task/Job
T1053.description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling mechanisms to facilitate one-time or recurring execution of malicious code.

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts.

T1021.name: Remote Services
T1021.description: Adversaries may use valid credentials to log into a service on another system.

T1114.name: Email Collection
T1114.description: Adversaries may target user email repositories to collect sensitive information.

T1486.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
T1486.description: Adversaries may encrypt data on victim systems to disrupt availability of system and network resources.

T1048.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
T1048.description: Adversaries may exfiltrate data using alternative protocols compared to standard command and control channels.

T1558.003.name: Kerberoasting
T1558.003.description: Adversaries may abuse Kerberos ticket requests to obtain passwords for offline cracking.

T1003.006.name: DCSync
T1003.006.description: Adversaries may request Active Directory replication data to obtain password hashes.

T1621.name: MFA Fatigue
T1621.description: Adversaries may bomb users with MFA requests until they accept.
